sourceDesc.p: Derived from CDSL MW, PWG, PWK, AP90, and GRA source records for interoperability analysis.

projectDesc.p: tei-archival-profile-v0.1; TEI is used as the archival/textual model. Raw CDSL records are preserved as escaped source-entry quotes, while selected lexical structures are promoted into TEI entry, form, sense, citation, and relation elements.

revisionDesc.change: Included in the full machine-reviewed TEI profile.

sense.def: drive, impel, animate (as a car or horse)
offer (as a hymn to the gods)
be pleased with, like, accept favourably (as sacrifices, prayers or hymns)

listBibl.head: Machine-extracted citation index; source quotes preserve the archival text.

cit.quote:
<L>16938<pc>96,1<k1>av<k2>av<e>1
<s>av</s> ¦ <ab>cl.</ab> 1. <ab>P.</ab> <s>a/vati</s> (<ab>Imper.</ab> 2. <ab>sg.</ab> <s>avatAt</s>, <ls>RV. viii, 2, 3</ls>, <ab>p.</ab> <s>a/vat</s>; <ab>impf.</ab> <s>A/vat</s>, 2. <ab>sg.</ab> <hom>1.</hom> <s>A/vaH</s> [for <hom>2.</hom> <s>A/vaH</s> See √ <s>vf</s>]; <ab>perf.</ab> 3. <ab>sg.</ab> <s>Ava</s>, 2. <ab>pl.</ab> <s>Ava/</s>, <ls>RV. viii, 7, 18</ls>, 2. <ab>sg.</ab> <s>A/viTa</s>; <ab>aor.</ab> <s>a/vIt</s>, 2. <ab>sg.</ab> <s>a/vIs</s>, <s>avIs</s> and <s>avizas</s>, <ab>Imper.</ab> <s>avizwu</s>, 2. <ab>sg.</ab> <s>aviqQi/</s> [once, <ls>RV. ii, 17, 8</ls>] or <s>aviqQi</s> [six times in <ls>RV.</ls>], 2. <ab>du.</ab> <s>avizwam</s>, 3. <ab>du.</ab> <s>avizwAm</s>, 2. <ab>pl.</ab> <s>avizwa/nA</s>, <ls>RV. vii, 18, 25</ls> <ab>Prec.</ab> 3. <ab>sg.</ab> <s>avyAs</s>, <ab>Inf.</ab> <s>a/vitave</s>, <ls>RV. vii, 33, 1</ls>; <lang>Ved.</lang> <ab>ind.p.</ab> <s>AvyA</s>, <ls>RV. i, 166, 13</ls>)
<div n="to"/>to drive, impel, animate (as a car or horse), <ls>RV.</ls>;
<div n="to"/> <lang>Ved.</lang> to promote, favour, (chiefly <lang>Ved.</lang>) to satisfy, refresh;
<div n="to"/>to offer (as a hymn to the gods), <ls>RV. iv, 44, 6</ls>;
<div n="to"/>to lead or bring to (<ab>dat.</ab>: <s>Uta/ye</s>, <s>vA/ja-sAtaye</s>, <s>kzatrA/ya</s>, <s>svasta/ye</s>), <ls>RV.</ls>;
<div n="to"/> (said of the gods) to be pleased with, like, accept favourably (as sacrifices, prayers or hymns), <ls>RV.</ls>;
<div n="to"/> (chiefly said of kings or princes) to guard, defend, protect, govern, <ls>BhP.</ls>; <ls>Ragh. ix, 1</ls>; <ls>VarBṛS.</ls> &c.:
<div n="vp"/><ab>Caus.</ab> (only <ab>impf.</ab> <s>Avayat</s>, 2. <ab>sg.</ab> <s>Avayas</s>) to consume, devour, <ls>RV.</ls>; <ls>AV.</ls>; <ls>VS.</ls>; <ls>ŚBr.</ls> <info whitneyroots="av,4"/><info verb="genuineroot" cp="1P"/>
<LEND>

<L>6233<pc>1-0465<k1>av<k2>av
{#av#}¦
<lang n="greek">(ἄω, aueo)</lang>, {#a/vati#}
<ls>DHĀTUP. 15,91.</ls> {#Avizam, AvIt#}; <ab>imperat.</ab> <ab>ved.</ab> {#aviqQi/, avizwu/, avizwa/#} ({#avita/#}); {#Ava; avizya/ti#}; <ab>inf.</ab> <ab>ved.</ab> {#a/vitave#}; <ab>gerund.</ab> <ab>ved.</ab> {#A/vya#}; <ab>partic.</ab> {#Uta#} (in {#tvota#}) und {#avita#} . Das {#a#} in {#avyAt#} <ab>u.</ab> {#avantu#} angeblich nicht <ab>elid.</ab> im <is>Veda</is>
<ls>P. 6,1,116.</ls>
<div n="1"> 1) {%Freude haben, sich gütlich thun, sich sättigen an Etwas%} (<ab>loc.</ab>)
<ls>NAIGH. 2,8.</ls> {#made^zu vfzannu\Sijo\ yadAvi^Ta#}
<ls>ṚV. 1,131,5.</ls> {#u\ta no\ brahma^nnavizA u\kTezu^#}
<ls n="ṚV.">3,13,6.</ls> {#karma^su no 'vata#}
<ls>VS. 20,74.</ls> {#A ca^ no ba\rhiH sada^tAvi\tA ca^ na spA\rhARi\ dAta^ve\ vasu^#}
<ls>ṚV. 7,59,6.</ls> {#proTa\daSvo\ na yava^se 'vi\zyan#}
<ls n="ṚV. 7,">3,2.</ls> {#tf\zva^vi\zyanna^ta\sezu^ tizWati#}
<ls n="ṚV.">1,58,2.</ls> <ls n="ṚV.">8,36,2.</ls> <ls n="ṚV.">10,15,1.</ls> <ls n="ṚV. 10,">120,7.</ls>
<div n="1">— 2) {%Jemand wohlthun, gütlich thun, sättigen%}: {#yatpa\rjanya^H pfTi\vIM reta\sAva^ti#} (<ab>vgl.</ab>
<ls>AV. 8,7,21</ls>) <ls>ṚV. 5,83,4.</ls> {#mi\trAvaru^RO tvA\ vfzwyA^vatAm#}
<ls>VS. 2,16.</ls> {#i\tTA su\taH pO\ra indra^mAva#}
<ls>ṚV. 2,11,11.</ls> {#Ava\dindra^M ya\munA\ tftsa^vaSca#}
<ls n="ṚV.">7,18,19.</ls> {#A De\navo^ mAmate\yamava^ntIrbrahma\priya^M pIpayan#}
<ls n="ṚV.">1,152,6.</ls> {#na me^ dU\rAdavi^tave\ vasi^zWAH#} {%mögen die%} <is>Vasiṣṭha</is> {%nie fern von mir sein, um mir gütlich zu thun%} (<is>Indra</is> spricht)
<ls n="ṚV.">7,33,1.</ls> {#(annaM) yadBUyo hinasti tadyatkanIyo na tadavati#} {%zu viel%} (Speise) {%schadet, zu wenig sättigt nicht%}
<ls>ŚAT. BR. 7,2,2,17.</ls> {#yadagnO juhvati tadenAnavati#}
<ls n="ŚAT. BR.">1,3,3,16.</ls> <ls n="ŚAT. BR. 1,">4,1,4.</ls> <ls n="ŚAT. BR. 1,">6,3,37.</ls> <ls n="ŚAT. BR.">7,5,1,14.</ls> <ls n="ŚAT. BR.">8,2,1,5.</ls> <ls n="ŚAT. BR.">10,4,1,3.</ls> <ls n="ŚAT. BR. 10,4,1,">14.</ls> <ls n="ŚAT. BR. 10,4,1,">20.</ls> {#ahamannam — ahamannAdaH — yo mA dadAti sa ideva mA3vAH#}
<ls>TAITT. UP. 3,10,6.</ls> {#saha nAvavatu . saha nO Bunaktu . saha vIryaM karavAvahE#}
<ls n="TAITT. UP.">3,11.</ls> {#kzatriyAntakaraRo 'pi vikramastena mAmavati#} ({%befriedigt%}) {#nAjite tvayi#}
<ls>RAGH. 11,75.</ls>
<div n="1">— 3) {%gern haben, wünschen, lieben%}: {#taM ra^kzatA maruto\ yamAva^ta#}
<ls>ṚV. 1,168,8.</ls> <ls n="ṚV. 1,168,">13.</ls> {#na mfzA^ SrA\ntaM yadava^nti de\vAH#}
<ls n="ṚV. 1,">179,3.</ls> {#sa\dyo ha^ jA\to vf^za\BaH ka\nIna\H praBa^rtumAva\danDa^saH su\tasya^#}
<ls n="ṚV.">3,48,1.</ls> {#viSvA\ hi mA\yA ava^si#}
<ls n="ṚV.">6,58,1.</ls> {#yatsa\tyaM ya^ta\radfjI^ya\staditsomo^ 'vati\ hantyAsa^t#}
<ls n="ṚV.">7,104,12.</ls> {#na mAmavati sadvIpA ratnasUrapi medinI ..#}
<ls>RAGH. 1,65.</ls> {#avita#} <lex>n.</lex> {%Gefallen, Freude%}, <ab>s.</ab> {#adroGAvita#} . —
[Page1-0466]
<div n="1">— 4) {%an Etwas Gefallen finden, sich Etwas angelegen sein lassen, beachten%}: {#ava^ta\M Diya^M me#}
<ls>ṚV. 2,40,5.</ls> {#a\smA~ a^vantu te\ Diya^H#}
<ls n="ṚV.">8,3,1.</ls> {#naro\ yadvA^maSvinA\ stoma\mAva^n#}
<ls n="ṚV.">4,44,7.</ls> {#a\vi\zwanA^ pEjava\nasya\ keta^m#}
<ls n="ṚV.">7,18,25.</ls> {#a\yA Di\yA mana^ve Sru\zwimAvyA^ sA\kaM naro^ da\MsanE\rA ci^kitrire#}
<ls n="ṚV.">1,166,13.</ls> <ls n="ṚV. 1,">129,4.</ls> <ls n="ṚV. 1,">182,4.</ls> <ls n="ṚV.">2,12,14.</ls> <ls n="ṚV.">3,8,8.</ls> <ls n="ṚV. 3,">62,8.</ls> <ls n="ṚV.">4,33,3.</ls> <ls n="ṚV.">7,64,5.</ls> <ls n="ṚV.">8,81,15.</ls> <ls n="ṚV. 8,">85,13.</ls> <ls>VS. 29,8.</ls> <ls>AV. 5,27,8.</ls> <ls n="AV.">7,20,5.</ls> <ls>SV. I,2,1,2,2.</ls> {#devacittaM tvAvatu mA manuzyacittam#}
<ls>ŚAT. BR. 11,8,3,9.</ls> <ls n="ŚAT. BR.">1,7,4,21.</ls> <ls n="ŚAT. BR.">2,1,3,2.</ls>
<div n="1">— 5) {%begünstigen, fördern, ermuthigen, helfen, schützen%}: {#sa pa^vasva\ ya Avi\Tendra^M vf\trAya\ hanta^ve#}
<ls>ṚV. 9,61,22.</ls> {#ya\jYena^ ya\jYama^va ya\jYiya\H sanya\jYaste\ vajra^mahi\hatya^ Avat#}
<ls n="ṚV.">3,32,12.</ls> {#a\nyA vo^ a\nyAma^vatu#}
<ls n="ṚV.">10,97,14.</ls> {#a\vi\qQI^ndra ci\trayA^ na U\tI#}
<ls n="ṚV.">2,17,8.</ls> {#a\smA~ a^va maGava\ngoma^ti vra\je#}
<ls n="ṚV.">8,59,6.</ls> <ls n="ṚV.">1,23,12.</ls> <ls n="ṚV. 1,">24,3.</ls> <ls n="ṚV. 1,">27,7.</ls> <ls n="ṚV. 1,">76,2.</ls> <ls n="ṚV. 1,">110,9.</ls> <ls n="ṚV. 1,">152,6.</ls> <ls n="ṚV. 1,">181,7.</ls> <ls n="ṚV. 1,">185,4.</ls> <ls n="ṚV. 1,185,">9.</ls> <ls n="ṚV.">2,12,14.</ls> <ls n="ṚV. 2,">30,6.</ls> <ls n="ṚV.">8,9,5.</ls> <ls n="ṚV.">9,83,2.</ls> <ls n="ṚV.">10,80,3.</ls> <ls n="ṚV. 10,">108,2.</ls> <ls>VS. 2,9.</ls> <ls>AV. 2,13,5.</ls> <ls n="AV.">3,19,5.</ls> <ls n="AV.">4,29,3.</ls> <ls n="AV.">14,1,35.</ls> <ls n="AV.">19,72,1.</ls> <ls>ŚAT. BR. 1,8,1,28.</ls> <ls>NIR. 3,9.</ls> {#satyaM vadizyAmi . tanmAmavatu . tadvaktAramavatu#}
<ls>TAITT. UP. 1,1. 12.</ls> {#pratyakzABiH prasannastunaBiravatu vastABirazwABirISaH#}
<ls>ŚĀK. 1.</ls> {#yamavatAmavatAM#} ({%der Schützenden, Regierenden%}) {#Duri sTitaH#}
<ls>RAGH. 9,1.</ls> {#avita#} {%beschützt, erhalten%}
<ls>AK. 3,2,55.</ls>
<ls>H. 1497.</ls> Im
<ls>DHĀTUP.</ls> werden dieser Wurzel folgende Bedeutungen gegeben: {#rakzaRa, gati#} (<ab>vgl.</ab>
<ls>NAIGH. 2,14</ls>), {#kAnti, prIti, tfpti, avagama, praveSa, SravaRa, svAmyarTa#} (Andere: {#sAmarTya#}), {#yAcana#} (oder {#arTana), kriyA, icCA#} (oder {#kAma#}), {#dIpti, avApti, AliNgana, hiMsA, AdAna#} (Andere: {#dahana), BAva#} (Andere: {#BAga), vfdDi#};
<ls>KĀTANTRA</ls> nur: {#pAlana#} . — <ab>caus.</ab> {#Avayati#} (die Betonung {#Ava^yati#}
<ls>NAIGH. 2,8</ls> scheint auf unrichtiger Ableitung von {#vI#} mit {#A#} zu beruhen; die Texte haben nur <ab>imperf.</ab>-Formen) {%verzehren%}: {#e\vAre^ vfzaBA su\te 'si^nva\nBUryA^vayaH#}
<ls>ṚV. 8,45,38.</ls> {#a\gnirna jamBE^stf\zvanna^mAvayat#}
<ls n="ṚV.">10,113,8.</ls> {#su\pa\rRastvA^ ga\rutmA\nviza^ praTa\mamA^vayat#}
<ls>AV. 4,6,3.</ls> {#avi^sto\kAnyA^vayat#}
<ls n="AV.">5,19,2.</ls> {#me\zasya^ ha\viza\ Ava^yat#}
<ls>VS. 21,44.</ls>
<ls>ŚAT. BR. 1,6,3,5.</ls> <ls n="ŚAT. BR.">5,5,4,6.</ls>
<div n="p">— {#anu#} {%aufmuntern, ermuthigen%}: {#anu^ tri\tasya\ yuDya^ta\H Suzma^mAvannu\ta kratu^m#}
<ls>ṚV. 8,7,24.</ls>
<div n="p">— {#ud#}
<div n="1"> 1) {%beachten, auf Etwas merken%}: {#Bage\mAM Diya\muda^vA\ dada^nnaH#}
<ls>ṚV. 7,41,3.</ls>
<div n="1">— 2) {%lauern%}: {#u\dava^ntO\ vfkA^viva#}
<ls>AV. 7,95,2.</ls>
<div n="1">— 3) {%fördern, antreiben%}: {#a\smAka\maMSa\muda^vA\ Bare^ Bare#}
<ls>ṚV. 1,102,4.</ls> {#indra\ udA^vatpati\maGnyA^nAm#}
<ls n="ṚV.">10,102,7.</ls>
<div n="p">— {#upa#} {%liebkosen, freundlich thun, ermuthigen%}; mit <ab>dat.</ab> oder <ab>acc.</ab>: {#vf\zA\ra\vAya\ vada^te yadu\pAva^ti vicci\kaH#}
<ls>ṚV. 10,146,2.</ls> {#kiM svi^tpu\treBya^H pi\tarA\ upA^vatuH#}
<ls n="ṚV.">1,161,10.</ls> {#pu\tro mA\tarA^ vi\cara\nnupA^vasi#}
<ls n="ṚV.">10,140,2.</ls> {#a\nyAnyasyA\ upA^vata#}
<ls n="ṚV. 10,">97,14.</ls> {#gAva\ upA^vAtAva\tam#}
<ls n="ṚV.">8,61,12.</ls> {#sIsA\yADyA^ha\ va^ruRa\H sIsA^yA\gnirupA^vati#}
<ls>AV. 1,16,2.</ls> {#upa hi dvitIyo 'vati#}
<ls>ŚAT. BR. 3,7,3,9.</ls>
<div n="p">— {#pra#}
<div n="1"> 1) {%beachten, auf Etwas merken%}: {#vi\SvA Diyo^ aSvinA\ prAva^taM naH#}
<ls>ṚV. 1,117,23.</ls> {#pra su^nva\ta stu^va\taH SaMsa^mAvaH#}
<ls n="ṚV. 1,">33,7.</ls> <ls n="ṚV. 1,">31,8.</ls> <ls n="ṚV.">10,26,1.</ls> <ls n="ṚV. 10,">97,17.</ls> {#prAva^ me\ vaca^H#}
<ls>AV. 13,1,42.</ls>
<div n="1">— 2) {%sich Jemandes annehmen, ermuntern, zu Etwas verhelfen%}: {#indra^H sa\matsu\ yaja^mAna\mArya\M prAva^t#}
<ls>ṚV. 1,130,8.</ls> {#prAva\nvARI^H puruhU\taM Dama^ntIH#}
<ls n="ṚV.">3,30,10.</ls> {#prAvI\nnu vI\ro ja^ri\tAramU\tI#}
<ls n="ṚV.">7,20,2.</ls> <ls n="ṚV.">1,4,8.</ls> <ls n="ṚV. 1,">33,14.</ls> <ls n="ṚV. 1,">36,17.</ls> <ls n="ṚV. 1,">47,5.</ls> <ls n="ṚV. 1,">51,5.</ls> <ls n="ṚV. 1,">81,1.</ls> <ls n="ṚV.">2,15,9.</ls> <ls n="ṚV.">7,19,3.</ls> <ls n="ṚV.">8,3,12.</ls> <ls n="ṚV. 8,">49,10.</ls> <ls n="ṚV.">10,103,7.</ls> {#(agniH) yami\mA viSa\H prAva^ntu jU\taye^#}
<ls n="ṚV.">1,127,2.</ls> <ls n="ṚV. 1,">102,3.</ls> <ls n="ṚV.">5,46,7.</ls> {#ya\jYaM prAva^ntu naH Su\Be#}
<ls>VS. 18,76.</ls> {#tAstvA^ putra\vidyA^ya\ dEvI\H prAva\ntvoza^DayaH#}
<ls>AV. 3,23,6.</ls> {#i\yamindra\M varu^Ramazwa me\ gIH prAva^tto\ke tana^ye\ tUtu^jAnA#}
<ls>ṚV. 7,84,5.</ls>
<div n="1">— 3) {%befriedigen, sättigen%}: {#yaddevAso^ la\lAma^gu\M pra vi^zwI\mina\mAvi^zuH#}
<ls>VS. 23,29.</ls> {#me\GAH prAva^ntu pfTi\vImanu^#}
<ls>AV. 4,15,9.</ls>
[Page1-0467]
<div n="p">— {#saMpra#} {%behülflich sein%}: {#Di\ye sama^SvinA\ prAva^taM vaH#}
<ls>AV. 6,4,3.</ls>
<div n="p">— {#sam#} {%befriedigen, sättigen%}: {#manu\M SUra^mi\zA sa\mAva^tam#}
<ls>ṚV. 1,112,18.</ls> {#saM vo^ 'vantu su\dAna^va\ utsA^H#}
<ls>AV. 4,15,7.</ls> <ls n="AV. 4,15,">9.</ls>
<LEND>
<L>212914<pc>7-310-b<k1>av<k2>av √{#av#}¦ I. {#avita#} <ls>R. ed. Bomb. 4,60,20</ls> nach dem <ab>Comm.</ab> = {#jYAta#}. <div n="p">— Mit {#aBi#} I. 5.<info n="sup_7"/> <LEND>

<L>4340<pc>0175-b<k1>av<k2>av {#av#}¦ {c1c} <ab>P.</ab> [{#avati, Ava, AvIt#}, <lbinfo n="avi#zyati"/> {#avizyati, avituM, avita#} or {#Uta#}] {@1@} To <lbinfo n="pro+tect"/> protect, defend; {#yamavatAmavatAM ca Duri#} <lbinfo n="sTi#taH"/> {#sTitaH#} <ls>R. 9. 1</ls>; {#pratyakzABiH#} <lbinfo n="prapannastanuBirava#tu"/> {#prapannastanuBiravatu vastABirazwABirISaH#} <ls>Ś. 1. 1.</ls> {@--2@} To please, satisfy, give pleasure to; do good to; {#vikramastena mAmavati nAjite tvayi#} <ls>R. 11. 75</ls>; {#na mAmavati sadvIpA#} <lbinfo n="ratnasUra#pi"/> {#ratnasUrapi medinI#}. <ls n="R.">1. 65.</ls> {@--3@} To like, wish, <lbinfo n="de+sire"/> desire, love. {@--4@} To favour, promote, animate. (In the Dhātupāṭha <lbinfo n="se+veral"/> several other meanings are assigned to this root, but they are very rarely used in classical literature; {%<ab>e. g.</ab>%} {#gati, kAMti, avagama, praveSa, SravaRa#}, <lbinfo n="svA#myarTa"/> {#svAmyarTa#} or {#sAmarTya, yAcana, kriyA, dIpti, avApti#}, {#grahaRa, vyApti, AliMgana, hiMsA, AdAna, dahana#}, {#BAva, BAga#} and {#vfdDi#}). {%--<ab>Caus.</ab>%} To <lbinfo n="con+sume"/> consume, devour. --WITH {#anu#} to <lbinfo n="en+courage"/> encourage, inspire. {#--ud#} {1} to regard, attend to. {2} to wait for. {3} to <lbinfo n="pro+mote"/> promote, impel. {#--upa#} {1} to cherish, behave friendly towards. {2} to <lbinfo n="en+courage"/> encourage. {#--saM#} {1} to satisfy, satiate. {2} to protect, maintain. [<ab>cf.</ab> <ab>L.</ab> {%aveo%}]. <LEND>

<L>1101<pc>0121<k1>av<k2>av {@√av,@}¦ stets mit dem <ab>Acc.</ab>, der jedoch an einzelnen wenigen Stellen ({502,6}; {231,6}; {655,11}; {441,7}; {600,5}; {451,6}; {600,5}; {946,7}; {562,2}; {185,4}) aus dem Zusammenhange zu ergänzen ist. Die sinnliche Grundbedeutung, soweit sie sich noch nachweisen lässt, ist „fördern“, genauer: etwas, was sich bewegt oder zu bewegen strebt, in den rechten (gewünschten, oder der Sache angemessenen) Zustand der Bewegung versetzen. In dieser sinnlichen Bedeutung erscheint es mit dem Objecte Wagen ({389,7}. _{389,8}; {689,6}; {222,1}; {112,12}. _{112,22}; {622,35}) oder Ross ({112,21}. _{112,22}; {269,3}), ähnlich in Verbindung mit prá ({102,3}; {689,4}; {928,1}; {326,6}), úd ({222,2}); dann auf geistiges [Page122] Gebiet übertragen „jemand fördern, unterstützen, begünstigen, ermuthigen, laben, erquicken“, und auf Dinge bezogen „etwas gern haben, lieben, etwas (Dargebotenes) gern annehmen, sich munden lassen“. Das <ab>Caus.</ab> bedeutet „essen, geniessen“, Also 1〉 {%fördern, antreiben%} (Rosse, Wagen, Wogen, Götter zur Eile); 2〉 jemand {%fördern, begünstigen, laben, erquicken, unterstützen,%} ihm {%hold sein,%} besonders von dem Beistande im Kampfe, und zwar Götter als Subject, Menschen als Object; 3〉 ebenso in der Beziehung der Götter auf Götter oder Gegenstände; 4〉 jemandem [<ab>A.</ab>] wozu [<ab>D.</ab>, <ab>L.</ab>] {%verhelfen;%} 5〉 {%laben, erquicken,%} Subject: Lieder, Speisen <ab>u. s. w.</ab>, Object: Götter <ab>u. s. w.</ab>; 6〉 etwas [<ab>A.</ab>] {%gern haben, lieben%} (von Göttern); 7〉 {%gern annehmen, sich wohl gefallen lassen,%} <ab>Subj.</ab>: Götter, <ab>Obj.</ab>: Opfer, Gebete, Lieder; 8〉 den Göttern [<ab>D.</ab>] Loblied [<ab>A.</ab>] {%zusenden;%} 9〉 <ab>Caus.</ab>, {%essen, verzehren%}. <div n="Pf">Mit {@ánu,@} {%erlaben, auffrischen%} (die Kraft). <div n="Pf">{@abhí,@} erquicken [<ab>A.</ab>]. <div n="Pf">{@úd@} 1〉 {%fördern, antreiben%} (Wagen); 2〉 {%fördern, unterstützen%} (Götter die Menschen); 3〉 {%gnädig annehmen%} (Gebete). <div n="Pf">{@úpa,@} mit <ab>Dat.</ab> oder <ab>Acc.</ab> 1〉 {%liebkosen;%} 2〉 {%erfrischen%}. <div n="Pf">{@prá@} 1〉 {%fördern, antreiben%} (Rosse, Wagen <ab>u. s. w.</ab>); 2〉 {%fördern, unterstützen%} (Götter die Menschen oder andere Götter); 3〉 {%gern annehmen%} (Gebete); 4〉 {%laben%} (Lieder die Götter). <div n="Pf">{@sám@} 1〉 {%erlaben%} (mit Speise); 2〉 {%zusammentreiben%} (zum Kampfe). <div n="H">Stamm {@áva:@} <div n="TS">-asi 2〉 súṣvim {464,2}. — 3〉 devā́n {656,3}. — 4〉 kṣatrā́ya tvam {657,6}. — 6〉 māyā́s {499,1}. <div n="TS">-ati 1〉 {622,35}. — 3〉 pṛthivī́m {437,4} (rétasā). — {@upa@} 1〉 vṛsāravā́ya vádate {972,2} (ciccikás). <div n="TS">-athas 1〉 {112,22}. — 2〉 {112,17}. _{112,20}; {417,1}; {440,1}; {585,4}. — 7〉 dhíyas {112,2}. <div n="TS">-atha [-athā] 2〉 {332,5}; {333,6}; {507,8}; {861,14}; {889,14}; {893,11}. — 3〉 síndhum {640,24} (marutas). <div n="TS">-anti 2〉 {179,3}; {795,2}. — 6〉 yád {214,19}. <div n="TS">-ās 2〉 yám {27,7}. <div n="TS">-āthas 7〉 yásya bráhmāṇi {577,2}. <div n="TS">-ātha 2〉 yám {556,3}. <div n="TS">-es {@prá@} 3〉 dhíyas {641,12}. <div n="TS">-et 2〉 ugrám {488,15}. — {@sám@} 2〉 jánau {388,8}. <div n="TS">-a [-ā] 1〉 {689,6}. — 2〉 {79,7};
